channel.title: Drupal Canvas
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Canvas.

item.title: #3591667: Add `canvas:doctor` Drupal CLI command to check health of Canvas' data: validate every Canvas (content|config) entity + translation, validate every non-Canvas content entity component tree field revision+translation, and more
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical unknown (0)
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591667 · 1 contributor · 5 comments

Summary

The Canvas module now ships a canvas:doctor command (requiring Drupal 11.4's dr CLI) that validates all Canvas data and reports integrity problems. It runs nine distinct health checks: four system checks (Canvas update paths executed, config entities that escaped the update path, whether all installed extensions are at tagged releases, and whether every component source plugin supports schema evolution) and five data checks (Canvas config entities, default content revisions, past revisions, forward/draft revisions, and auto-save snapshots). Each check reports a count of healthy vs. failing items, a duration, and optional per-violation details with a prescription for remediation. The command can output a human-readable table or structured JSON. Validation also runs incrementally via cron on sites that never use the CLI, so health records stay warm without requiring manual intervention.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: New canvas_health_records table is created lazily on first write; no update hook is required.
  • New API: New canvas:doctor CLI command (requires Drupal 11.4+) with --checks, --all-checks, --no-cache, --details, and --format=json options for auditing Canvas data integrity.

Technical details

The implementation is organized into five @internal classes under src/Health/ plus the command itself in src/Command/DoctorCommand.php.

HealthCheck is a string-backed PHP enum with nine cases split into system checks (run once per invocation, never cached) and data checks (streamed as generators, results persisted). The isSystemCheck() and findingsAreRisks() methods are the single source of truth for those splits: adding a new case without updating both exhaustive match expressions causes a PHPStan failure.

Doctor is the validation engine. runDataChecks() yields CheckItem structs via generators, which lets DoctorCommand display a live progress bar without loading all entities into memory first. runCronWindow() accepts an offset and limit, enabling HealthHooks::cron() to advance a cursor stored in Drupal state across separate cron runs. Each data check's freshness signal is computed at the moment Doctor visits the item, not when Inventory listed it, so paginated results from a previous cron run do not carry stale freshness data.

HealthRecords persists results in the canvas_health_records database table (keyed on data_key + check). The table is created lazily on first write rather than via a formal install hook. Freshness is determined by matching two fingerprints: an environment fingerprint (Canvas schema version, count of applied and pending post-updates, xxh64 hash of all installed extension versions) computed by Environment, and a data fingerprint per item (cache-tag checksum for config and content entities, a precomputed data hash for auto-save snapshots). If any installed extension reports no release version (dev), HealthRecords::isFresh() unconditionally returns false, disabling all result reuse.

UpdatesEscapedConfig detection uses reflection to enumerate CanvasConfigUpdater::needs*() methods, filters to those whose first required parameter type-matches the entity being inspected, and calls each. Deprecation emissions from those methods are suppressed during the audit by temporarily calling setDeprecationsEnabled(FALSE). The DoctorCommand itself is thin: it delegates all logic to Doctor so the same validation can power the planned web UI (#3588907 via Drupal Batch API).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 12, 2026

Key contributors

  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • phenaproxima (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591674: Explicitly support `comment` as a referenceable content entity type (stop omitting it from the content entity types endpoint)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591674 · 1 contributor · 4 comments

Summary

In the Canvas module, the content entity types API endpoint now correctly lists comment as a referenceable entity type for users who hold access comments and have view access to the parent entity type (e.g., access content for comments on nodes). Previously, comment was silently absent from the picker even when permissions were in place, because the access check always failed on the stub entity used for eligibility testing.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Canvas evaluates whether an entity type/bundle is referenceable by creating an unsaved stub entity and running a 'view' access check on it via getBundleViewAccess(). CommentAccessControlHandler::checkAccess() calls $entity->getCommentedEntity()->access('view'), which throws on a stub that has no commented entity. The error was caught by getBundleViewAccess() and treated as forbidden, so comment was never included.

The fix is in ApiUiContentEntityReferenceControllers::createBundleStub(). After creating a comment stub, the method now loads the comment type's targetEntityTypeId and creates a normalized stub of that parent type (using the same activation/publish normalization extracted into the new normalizeStub() helper), then injects it as the entity_id of the comment stub. This gives the access handler a valid parent to check. If the parent entity type has no bundles yet, or its provider module has been uninstalled leaving a dangling target_entity_type_id, the method returns NULL and the comment bundle is skipped cleanly. The $inject_commented_entity parameter defaults to TRUE but is passed as FALSE for the recursive parent-stub call to prevent infinite recursion on comment-on-comment setups.

getBundleViewAccess() remains as a last-resort catch for unexpected handler failures, but is no longer the primary path for comment. The test testContentEntityTypesCatchesStubAccessThrow() (which previously asserted comment was absent) is replaced by three tests: testCommentIsReferenceableWithPermissions() asserts comment appears in the listing and its fields endpoint returns 200; testCommentOmittedWithoutAccessCommentsPermission() and testCommentOmittedWithoutParentTypeViewAccess() pin the conjunction-gate behavior.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 15, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591130: Deleted (code) components can still be referenced by folders: dependency handling is missing
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +18/−0 · php +18
  • Discussion: #3591130 · 2 contributors · 9 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

When a Canvas component is renamed via the CLI, the operation is actually a delete followed by a create. Any Folder config entity that listed the old component in its items retained the stale name after the delete, because Canvas's dependency tracking did not know the connection existed. A later operation on that folder (such as a PATCH) would fail with a 422 "Unprocessable Content" error and a message like "The 'canvas.component.js.media-feature-grid' config does not exist." The fix adds proper config-dependency declarations to Folder so that Drupal's built-in dependency resolution removes deleted components from folder items automatically. Existing sites need to run database updates to recompute the previously missing dependencies.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Database update: New post-update function resaves all Folder entities to compute previously missing config dependencies; run drush updb after updating.

Technical details

The root cause is that Folder never overrode calculateDependencies(). It inherited ConfigEntityBase::preDelete() and ConfigEntityBase::onDependencyRemoval() unchanged, but those methods can only act on dependencies that have already been declared. Because no dependencies were declared, deleting or renaming a JavaScriptComponent (or any other config entity stored in a folder) left the Folder's items array untouched. The fix in !1133 implements Folder::calculateDependencies() to register each item in items as a config dependency. Once those dependencies are present in the config graph, ConfigEntityBase::onDependencyRemoval() can fire and strip the deleted entry from items without any custom delete-hook logic. A post-update function (modeled on canvas_post_update_0002_intermediate_component_dependencies_in_patterns() and its companion UpdateIntermediateDependenciesUpdateTest) was added to resave all existing Folder entities so their dependency metadata is computed and stored. This MR (!1135) contains only the corresponding test in CanvasConfigEntityHttpApiTest, asserting that after an HTTP DELETE of a component, the folder's items list is empty. A related follow-up issue, #3591475, tracks the additional behavior of deleting the Folder itself when its last dependency is removed.

Upgrade

After updating the module, run database updates to recompute folder dependencies on existing sites:

drush updb
# or
vendor/bin/drush updb

Without this step, existing Folder config entities will continue to lack declared dependencies and the bug will persist for them even after the code update.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 20, 2026

Key contributors

  • bnjmnm (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

Collaboration

wim leers identified the underlying cause in comment #5: because Folder never implemented calculateDependencies(), Drupal's config dependency system had no record of the folder-to-component relationship, making all the inherited removal machinery inert. This reframed the fix from a bespoke delete-hook approach to a standard dependency-declaration approach. He also flagged the need for a database update path for existing sites, and generated it using an AI tool in a single prompt for $1.73 and roughly nine minutes.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3576837: Manage the global layout (aka regions) within Canvas Workbench
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 68 files, +3196/−509 · js +2827/−495 · mdx +140/−9 · php +92 · json +62/−3 · md +51/−2 · yml +24
  • Discussion: #3576837 · 5 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Canvas Workbench and the Canvas CLI now treat global regions (header, footer, etc.) as first-class items alongside pages and content templates. Developers place region JSON specs in a regionsDir directory, run canvas pull --include-regions or canvas push --include-regions to two-way sync them with Drupal, and optionally add a src/layout.jsx file that wraps Workbench page and content template previews with the surrounding regions. If the layout file is absent, previews continue rendering without surrounding regions, so existing projects are unaffected until they opt in.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: canvas_oauth_post_update_0006_page_region_scope installs a new canvas:page_region OAuth scope gated by the administer page template permission.
  • New API: New regionsDir and layoutPath config keys in canvas.config.json; new --include-regions CLI flag for pull, push, and validate; new ApiService methods listRegions, getRegion, createRegion, updateRegion, deleteRegion targeting /canvas/api/v0/config/page_region; Region component exported from drupal-canvas@^0.4.0.

Technical details

Two new keys are added to canvas.config.json: regionsDir (default ./regions) and layoutPath (default ./src/layout.jsx). Region spec files in regionsDir use the same element tree JSON format as page specs. The filename (without extension) becomes the region machine name; the Drupal theme is implicit and resolved server-side from the default theme when pushing. Push semantics are full-sync: remote regions absent from the local regionsDir are deleted.

The ApiService class gains listRegions(), getRegion(), createRegion(), updateRegion(), and deleteRegion() methods, all targeting the /canvas/api/v0/config/page_region REST endpoint. The --include-regions / CANVAS_INCLUDE_REGIONS=true option is wired into the pull, push, and validate commands. A new createRegionsPullTask() function and push utilities (prepareRegions, pushRegions, collectRegionResults, validateRegions) parallel the existing page and content-template paths.

A new canvas:page_region OAuth scope is installed by canvas_oauth_post_update_0006_page_region_scope, gated by the administer page template permission. The canvas_oauth authentication provider now protects PageRegion config entities alongside ContentTemplate, BrandKit, and others.

The layout file must export a default React component accepting children. The Region component from drupal-canvas (now at ^0.4.0) slots in a region by machine name; regions with no matching spec file render as nothing, and a fallback prop can supply a placeholder during development.

Internally, the authored-element-utils module is consolidated into a new authored-elements module that exposes shared helpers (componentTreeToAuthoredElementMap, authoredElementMapToComponentTree, jsonRenderSpecToAuthoredElementMap) used by all three entity types. A new ConfigComponentTreePayload type and stripNullableKeysForConfigComponentTree() function handle the fact that config-entity component trees (regions, content templates) reject null values for parent_uuid, slot, and label, while page content entities tolerate them. The now-redundant authoredPropToServer() pass-through (which was only a no-op for pages/regions) is removed from the content-template utilities.

Upgrade

After running database updates, the new OAuth scope is available:

drush updb

To opt in to region sync, add to canvas.config.json:

{
  "regionsDir": "./regions",
  "layoutPath": "./src/layout.jsx"
}

Create region specs in regions/:

// regions/header.json
{
  "status": true,
  "elements": {
    "header": {
      "type": "js.header",
      "props": { "darkVariant": true },
      "slots": { "branding": ["header-logo"] }
    },
    "header-logo": {
      "type": "js.logo",
      "props": { "linkToFrontPage": true }
    }
  }
}

Create src/layout.jsx to wrap Workbench previews with regions:

import { Region } from 'drupal-canvas';

export default function Layout({ children }) {
  return (
    <>
      <Region name="header" />
      <main>{children}</main>
      <Region name="footer" />
    </>
  );
}

Pull or push regions with the new flag:

npx canvas pull --include-regions
npx canvas push --include-regions

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 3, 2026

Key contributors

  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • balintbrews (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • rikki_iki
  • lauriii (Acquia)

Collaboration

The design discussion shaped the final approach significantly. effulgentsia proposed three options for how to surface regions in Workbench, and in comment #16522896 described a conversation with rikki_iki in which she suggested Option 2 (regions shown as part of page previews rather than in isolation) and specifically proposed using React Router concepts — a layout with an outlet — rather than Drupal's region terminology in the local codebase. wotnak pushed back in comment #16582626, arguing that purely using an outlet without a local concept of regions would make two-way CLI sync difficult, and proposed the hybrid approach that landed: local region JSON specs that sync like pages, combined with a local layout.jsx for Workbench rendering. wim leers flagged in comment #16584111 that making PageRegion implement CanvasHttpApiEligibleConfigEntityInterface carried significant security implications and requested it be extracted to a separate blocking issue, which became #3589801.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3540578: Adding components to folders with drag & drop
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 16 files, +242/−76 · js +213/−72 · css +29/−4
  • Discussion: #3540578 · 6 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

Drupal Canvas (formerly Experience Builder) now lets site builders organize components into folders using drag and drop in the Library panel. While the Library panel is open, the editor canvas dims and blurs to signal that drag operations target folders rather than the page layout. The work from this MR formed the foundation for the larger refactor in #3561018, where the feature ultimately landed.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New useUpdateFolderMutation hook exported from componentAndLayoutApi for PATCH operations on canvas/api/v0/config/folder/{id}

Technical details

A new FolderDragContext component wraps LibraryItemList in a DndContext from @dnd-kit/core. Its dragEndHandler checks whether the drop target carries destination: 'folders' in its data, then issues sequential updateFolder PATCH mutations: first removing the component from its prior folder (if any) by filtering it out of priorFolder.items, then appending it to the target folder's items array.

SidebarFolder gains a required id prop and registers itself as a droppable via useDroppable when activePanel === 'manageLibrary'. A new .isOver CSS rule applies a blue outline and background during hover. ListItem previously blocked all drag operations in the manageLibrary panel; that guard is removed, and the drag data's origin field is set to the active panel name rather than the hardcoded string 'library'.

The new updateFolder RTK Query mutation sends PATCH canvas/api/v0/config/folder/{id} and invalidates both Folders and Layout cache tags. It is excluded from withAutoSavesInjection alongside createFolder and createContentTemplate. EditorFrame and ContextualPanel each accept a new inactive boolean prop; when activePanel === 'manageLibrary', an absolutely-positioned overlay with backdrop-filter: blur(10px) and 30% black background covers the canvas. The sidebar panel labels are also renamed: the add-components panel changes from "Library" to "Add" and the folder-management panel from "Manage library" to "Library".

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: August 9, 2025

Key contributors

  • balintbrews (Acquia)
  • jessebaker (Acquia)

Collaboration

jessebaker spotted a gap in the issue description early on (credited by balintbrews). bnjmnm then implemented the initial drag-and-drop functionality in MR !116, noting in a comment the known gaps still remaining (no drag-out support, no loading indicator, no styling). The MR was ultimately closed rather than merged, with jessebaker noting it "formed the foundation" of the broader work that landed in #3561018.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3556101: [11.3.x] Semi-coupled engine not compatible with Drupal 11.x
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +321/−325 · php +315/−322 · yml +6/−3
  • Discussion: #3556101 · 6 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

Canvas's semi_coupled theme engine became incompatible with Drupal 11.3.x after core issue #1685492 converted theme engines from hook-based .engine files into tagged services. Any Canvas page on an 11.3.x site threw a 500 error with the message Service "semi_coupled" not found: the container inside "Drupal\Core\Theme\ThemeManager" is a smaller service locator that only knows about the "twig" service. This issue proposed converting the engine to comply with the new service-based API. It was ultimately closed as fixed when #3555062 removed the semi-coupled theme engine from Canvas entirely, making Canvas compatible with both 11.2.x and 11.3.x without needing the engine approach at all.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause is that Drupal 11.3.x (core #1685492) requires theme engines to be registered as tagged services implementing ThemeEngineInterface rather than as standalone .engine files providing procedural hooks (hook_extension(), hook_theme(), hook_render_template()). The old semi_coupled.engine file and its accompanying semi_coupled.info.yml (of type theme_engine) were incompatible with this model.

MR !296 proposes the intermediate fix: move all engine logic from procedural functions into a SemiCoupledEngine class implementing ThemeEngineInterface, and register it in canvas.services.yml with tags: [{name: theme_engine, engine_name: semi_coupled}]. The methods theme() and renderTemplate() map directly to the old semi_coupled_theme() and semi_coupled_render_template() hooks; the helper functions _semi_coupled_collect_props(), _semi_coupled_build_html_tag(), _semi_coupled_get_variable(), _semi_coupled_snake_case(), and _semi_coupled_kebab_case() become private methods on the class.

This MR was not merged. The actual fix arrived via #3555062, which removed the semi_coupled theme engine from Canvas entirely and improved React embedding without relying on a custom engine, resolving the 11.3.x incompatibility in a way that also kept 11.2.x working.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: November 5, 2025

Collaboration

alexpott identified a parallel fix in core (#3556130, "ThemeEngine BC layer is broken - ServiceLocator != Container") immediately after the issue was filed, which briefly raised the question of whether the Canvas side needed changing at all. wim leers and penyaskito clarified that Canvas targets 11.2.x as its minimum and would not require 11.3.x for 1.0, so the issue was postponed until the minimum version bumped. phenaproxima pointed out that landing #3555062 (removing the engine) would close this issue as outdated without needing the tagged-service migration at all, and that is ultimately what happened.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3555487: default-relative-url may crash with JSON storage
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3555487 · 2 contributors · 3 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

On MySQL 8.0, which stores JSON natively rather than as a string, the Canvas module can crash with a 500 error when rendering pages that use default prop values for image or video components. This caused the Byte demo home page of Drupal CMS 2.0 to fail on MySQL 8.0. The fix landed in the related issue #3550165.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The crash originates in Drupal\canvas\PropSource\DefaultRelativeUrlPropSource, which compares a stored JSON schema against a minimal expected shape using ksort() before an equality check. That one-level sort does not recurse into nested properties keys, so when MySQL 8.0's native JSON type returns property keys in a different order than MariaDB's string-encoded JSON, the comparison fails and an exception is thrown. Versions of MariaDB that DDEV defaults to store JSON as strings, preserving insertion order and masking the bug; MySQL 8.0 does not, exposing it. The fix, applied in #3550165, adds a second pass of ksort() over each entry inside properties for both the stored schema and the minimal schema before the equality check.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: October 31, 2025

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3551271: Add a button to add new pages from pages overview
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +66/−1 · php +36 · yml +30/−1
  • Discussion: #3551271 · 2 contributors · 2 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The Canvas module's pages overview at /admin/content/pages now has an "Add page" action button, matching the standard Drupal pattern for content overview pages. Clicking it immediately creates a new unpublished Canvas page titled "Untitled page" and redirects the user to its edit form. The button is only shown to users who have the create canvas_page entity access.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New route canvas.canvas_page.add at /admin/content/pages/add and named overview route canvas.canvas_pages_overview at /admin/content/pages.

Technical details

The change introduces a new PageController class in Drupal\canvas\Controller with a single addPage() method. The method calls Page::create() with title = 'Untitled page' and status = 0 (unpublished), saves the entity, then issues a RedirectResponse to entity.canvas_page.edit_form.

Two new routes are registered in canvas.routing.yml. The canvas.canvas_pages_overview route (path /admin/content/pages) formalises the existing overview as a named admin route protected by the edit canvas_page permission. The canvas.canvas_page.add route (path /admin/content/pages/add) points to PageController::addPage, enforces _entity_create_access: 'canvas_page:canvas_page', and requires a CSRF token. A local action link (canvas.add_canvas_page) is wired to appear on the overview route via canvas.links.action.yml, and a menu link entry for the overview is added to canvas.links.menu.yml under system.admin_content.

A minor typo was also corrected in canvas.permissions.yml: a stray backtick was appended to the "Delete Canvas Page" description.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: October 9, 2025

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3549124: Confirm overriding local global CSS file with the download command
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3549124 · 1 contributor · 2 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The Canvas CLI tool previously fetched global.css from Canvas silently in the background during build and upload, and the download command would overwrite the local global.css without warning. This issue, resolved via #3560957, introduces explicit control over global CSS syncing. The download and upload commands now list "Global CSS" as a selectable option in interactive mode (pre-selected by default). The build command switches to a local-first strategy: it reads the local global.css when it exists instead of always pulling from the remote API. New --skip-css and --css-only flags for both download and upload give non-interactive and CI/CD users precise control over whether and what CSS is transferred.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New --skip-css and --css-only flags on the download and upload CLI commands; getGlobalCss() and downloadGlobalCssInBackground() utility functions added to build-tailwind.ts

Technical details

The core change is a getGlobalCss(useLocal: boolean = true) function added to build-tailwind.ts. When useLocal is true and a local global.css exists under the configured CANVAS_COMPONENT_DIR, it is read from disk; otherwise the function falls back to downloadGlobalCssInBackground(), which calls apiService.getGlobalAssetLibrary() without touching the local file. Both buildComponent() in build.ts and buildTailwindForComponents() in build-tailwind.ts now accept a useLocalGlobalCss parameter (default true) and pass it through to getGlobalCss, replacing their previous direct calls to apiService.getGlobalAssetLibrary() for CSS.

The component-selector.ts module gains a GLOBAL_CSS_SELECTOR sentinel string and a determineGlobalCssSelection() helper. Both selectLocalComponents() and selectRemoteComponents() return an includeGlobalCss boolean in their result types (LocalSelectorResult and RemoteSelectorResult). In interactive mode, a "Global CSS" option is inserted immediately after "All components" and pre-selected when globalCssDefault is true. The --css-only path short-circuits component discovery entirely and returns an empty component list with includeGlobalCss: true.

The download command now fetches components and global CSS in parallel via Promise.all (unless --css-only is set, in which case only CSS is fetched), and writes the local global.css only when includeGlobalCss is true. The upload command skips the global CSS upload when includeGlobalCss is false, and passes includeGlobalCss through getBuildAndUploadResults to control which CSS source is used for the Tailwind build. An inline @todo workaround that re-fetched the existing css.original from the API before every upload (to avoid overwriting it with an empty value) was removed, since getGlobalCss() now provides the correct local source.

Upgrade

CLI users and CI/CD scripts that call npx canvas build, npx canvas download, or npx canvas upload should note the behavior changes:

  • build and upload now use the local global.css by default instead of fetching CSS from Canvas on every run. Ensure your local global.css is up to date before building.
  • download now includes global CSS in the download by default. Use --skip-css to preserve the old skip behavior.
  • New flags for download and upload:
# Skip global CSS during download or upload
npx canvas download --all --skip-css
npx canvas upload --all --skip-css

# Transfer only global CSS, skip components
npx canvas download --css-only
npx canvas upload --css-only
  • --skip-css and --css-only cannot be combined; doing so throws an error.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 27, 2025

Collaboration

The design was shaped by a focused exchange between balintbrews and lauriii early in the thread. lauriii questioned whether silently compiling with the remote CSS when a user skips the global CSS upload would produce confusing "side effects," particularly in multi-user environments where one person edits via CLI and another edits in the browser code editor. In response, balintbrews updated the issue summary to adopt lauriii's preferred behavior: when global CSS upload is skipped, the build falls back to fetching the remote CSS in the background without touching the local file, making any differences in compiled output an explicit consequence of the user's choice rather than a hidden surprise.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3544517: Support SVG images
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3544517 · 2 contributors · 4 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

Experience Builder (canvas) previously validated image prop URLs by matching file extensions with a hardcoded regex that only permitted PNG, GIF, JPG, JPEG, WEBP, and AVIF. This excluded SVG and any other image format. The fix replaces that approach with the JSON Schema contentMediaType: image/* annotation, allowing any image MIME type. The same change applies to video props, which previously only allowed MP4. As a side effect, the system now properly maps image string props to the image field type rather than a plain string field type, which means image props in generated field-based UX components are backed by an image field with the image_image widget instead of a plain text field.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: JSON schema definitions image-uri and stream-wrapper-image-uri replace extension-regex pattern with contentMediaType: image/* and a simplified URL pattern; image and video prop shapes now map to field type image with widget image_image instead of string.

Technical details

The old json-schema-definitions://experience_builder.module/image-uri and stream-wrapper-image-uri definitions carried pattern regexes that explicitly enumerated case-insensitive extensions. Those are replaced with contentMediaType: image/* plus a simplified URL-structure pattern. The video prop definition similarly moves from an MP4-only extension regex to contentMediaType: video/*.

On the matching side, JsonSchemaFieldInstanceMatcher previously called buildCaseInsensitiveExtensionRegex() to synthesise extension-based Regex constraints for file URI field properties. That method is removed. Instead, a new fileExtensionsToTargetContentMediaType() helper uses the file.mime_type.guesser.extension service to map a media type's allowed extensions to their wildcard MIME category (e.g. ["jpg","png","svg"] → "image/*"), and attaches the new UriTargetMediaTypeConstraint instead.

UriTargetMediaTypeConstraint is a no-op validation constraint (modelled on Drupal core's FullyValidatable) whose only role is to carry MIME type semantics on a data definition, enabling the shape-matching logic in mediaLibraryStoragePropShapeAlter and JsonSchemaStringFormat::computeStorablePropShape() to recognise image and video URI props and wire them to the image field type with the image_image widget.

Supporting changes: DataTypeShapeRequirement and DataTypeShapeRequirements now implement \IteratorAggregate, letting JsonSchemaType spread either type uniformly. StorablePropShape::fieldTypeProp and CandidateStorablePropShape::fieldTypeProp now accept ReferenceFieldTypePropExpression in addition to FieldTypePropExpression|FieldTypeObjectPropsExpression. JsonSchemaType::patternToPcre() extracts the pattern-to-PCRE conversion that was previously inlined. The ordering of format vs pattern cases in JsonSchemaType::computeStorablePropShape() was corrected so that format takes precedence when both are present.

Upgrade

SDC prop schemas for image URLs must replace the old extension regex with contentMediaType and a URL-structure pattern:

# Before
src:
  type: string
  format: uri-reference
  pattern: '^(/|https?://)?.*\.([Pp][Nn][Gg]|[Gg][Ii][Ff]|[Jj][Pp][Gg]|[Jj][Pp][Ee][Gg]|[Ww][Ee][Bb][Pp]|[Aa][Vv][Ii][Ff])(\?.*)?(#.*)?$'

# After — or use $ref: json-schema-definitions://experience_builder.module/image-uri
src:
  type: string
  format: uri-reference
  contentMediaType: image/*
  pattern: '^(/|https?://)?(?!.*\://)[^\s]+$'

Stream-wrapper image URIs:

# Before
src:
  type: string
  format: uri
  pattern: '^(?!https?://)[\w\-]+://.*\.([Pp][Nn][Gg]|...)(\?.*)?(#.*)?$'

# After — or use $ref: json-schema-definitions://experience_builder.module/stream-wrapper-image-uri
src:
  type: string
  format: uri
  contentMediaType: image/*
  pattern: '^(?!https?://)[\w\-]+://'

Video URL props follow the same pattern with contentMediaType: video/*.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 3, 2025

Key contributors

  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

Collaboration

wim leers pointed out in the issue thread that the regex had already been removed in commit 9048ab8 as part of a broader refactor (with a linked change record at drupal.org/node/3542579), and asked larowlan to retest against HEAD. larowlan confirmed the issue was resolved after doing so.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3541033: Add ability to switch content used by template using top navigation
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 9 files, +347/−3 · js +340/−3 · css +7
  • Discussion: #3541033 · 5 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

When building or editing a content template in Canvas, site builders can now select which content entity to use for previewing that template directly from the top navigation bar. A new dropdown lists all available entities of the matching bundle; choosing one navigates the canvas to that entity. When no content exists yet for the template's bundle, a dedicated full-screen notice explains the situation and provides an "Add new {bundle}" button that opens Drupal's content creation UI in a new tab.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New useGetPreviewContentEntitiesQuery hook and PreviewContentEntitiesResponse type for listing preview-eligible entities by entity type and bundle.

Technical details

The TopBar component now conditionally renders a new ContentPreviewSelector dropdown on any route starting with /template/. It feeds the dropdown by calling useGetPreviewContentEntitiesQuery, a new RTK Query endpoint that hits canvas/api/v0/ui/content_template/suggestions/preview/{entityTypeId}/{bundle}. Selecting an item navigates to /template/:entityType/:bundle/:viewMode/:previewEntityId.

A new route /template/:entityType/:bundle/:viewMode (without previewEntityId) is registered alongside the existing :previewEntityId route. It renders the new TemplateRoot component, which on mount queries useGetContentTemplatesQuery and immediately redirects (with replace) to the suggested entity if one is available. When none is found, it renders the no-content notice; the "Add new" button constructs a URL as {baseUrl}{entityType}/add/{bundle} using getBaseUrl().

PageInfo gains template-route awareness: it shows a FileIcon and a caption of the form {bundleLabel} - {viewModeLabel} template, resolved by looking up bundle and view mode labels from the existing useGetContentTemplatesQuery response.

New types PreviewContentEntity and PreviewContentEntitiesResponse are added in the services file, and PreviewContentEntities is registered as a new RTK cache tag. The TemplateListItem link fallback is also corrected from 0 to an empty string for cases where suggestedPreviewEntityId is absent.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: August 12, 2025

Key contributors

  • mred9
  • jessebaker
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3539909: Access bypass - ApiLayoutController::componentInstanceExistInComponentData doesn't correctly recurse the tree
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3539909 · 2 contributors · 5 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

An access bypass existed in the Canvas page builder's layout API. When a PATCH request was made to update a component instance, the controller was supposed to check whether the user had edit access to the page region containing that component. Due to a flawed recursive tree search, the component could fail to be located, causing the access check to be silently skipped. This allowed an authenticated user to patch components in restricted page regions without the required permission.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Security: Access bypass in ApiLayoutController::patch() allowed updating components in restricted page regions without edit permission

Technical details

The bug lived in ApiLayoutController::componentInstanceExistInComponentData(). Its recursion was broken: it checked !empty($slotData) before examining the slot's child components, meaning slots without deeply nested children were never searched. The outer getRegionForComponentInstance() caller returned NULL when no region was found, and the access check in patch() was written as if ($regionForComponentId !== MAIN_CONTENT_REGION && NULL !== $regionForComponentId), so a NULL result bypassed the PageRegion::access('edit') guard entirely.

The fix landed as part of #3541057. The entire componentInstanceExistInComponentData / getRegionForComponentInstance pair was deleted and replaced with getEntityWithComponentInstance(), which delegates tree traversal to ComponentTreeItemList::getComponentTreeItemByUuid(). That method correctly walks the full component tree. The refactored patch() method calls getEntityWithComponentInstance() to identify which entity (or PageRegion) owns the target component, then performs a direct $entity_to_patch->access('edit') check on that entity before proceeding. A NotFoundHttpException is thrown if the UUID is absent from the tree, eliminating the silent-bypass path.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: August 6, 2025

Key contributors

  • larowlan (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3500761: Allow code components to import from npm packages
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3500761 · 5 contributors · 27 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

Drupal Canvas code components (in-browser-editable JSX components) currently cannot import third-party npm packages. This spike issue investigated whether import maps and a public ES module CDN could make that possible without a bundler. The team proved the approach viable and identified a simpler path than originally proposed: bare npm specifiers in the component source are rewritten to absolute esm.sh CDN URLs at compile time, with a static import map covering the React/Preact aliases. A predefined set of bundled packages was landed in a related issue (#3508734) as an interim step; support for arbitrary npm imports is being implemented in #3560197 using the approach validated here.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Code components will be able to use bare npm import specifiers (e.g. import * as X from "@radix-ui/react-accordion") which Canvas rewrites to esm.sh URLs at compile time.

Technical details

The original proposal called for using @jspm/generator in the browser to resolve transitive dependencies and generate a full import map, storing the resolved package list in js_imports and js_import_dependencies properties on the JsComponent config entity. A key discovery during the spike changed this plan: esm.sh rewrites all internal import specifiers within the packages it serves so that every dependency is also fetched from esm.sh. This means a component importing @radix-ui/react-accordion does not need its entire transitive dependency tree enumerated in the import map; only the top-level specifier needs to be present.

The simplified approach replaces a bare specifier like import * as Accordion from "@radix-ui/react-accordion" with import * as Accordion from "https://esm.sh/@radix-ui/react-accordion?external=react,react-dom" at compile time, alongside a static import map that maps react, react-dom, and preact to the Preact compat layer also served from esm.sh. This avoids runtime dependency resolution entirely and removes the need to store resolved package lists on the config entity for the initial implementation.

Parsing import statements from JSX source was tested with several tools. @jspm/generator's HTML parser rejected JSX syntax; acorn combined with acorn-jsx also failed on the sample component. babylon (the parser underlying Babel) succeeded and additionally provided syntax validation as a side-effect. A practical constraint also surfaced: browsers do not allow an existing page's import map to be mutated at runtime, so injecting an import map into a live preview requires an iframe boundary. CDN configurability and per-component version locking were explicitly deferred as follow-up work.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 19, 2025

Collaboration

balintbrews identified that esm.sh auto-rewrites all internal dependency imports within the packages it serves, which eliminated the need for @jspm/generator at serve time and made the full js_imports/js_import_dependencies config-entity proposal premature. This single observation collapsed a multi-step dependency-resolution pipeline into a simple URL substitution, and balintbrews backed it with a working POC (https://github.com/balintbrews/poc-import-from-esm-sh) demonstrating Preact, a React compat layer, and @radix-ui/react-accordion all imported from esm.sh via an import map. larowlan contributed the earlier POC showing that babylon could parse JSX import statements and feed them to @jspm/generator to produce import maps, and flagged the runtime immutability constraint on import maps that implies iframe sandboxing for live previews.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3468944: Update XB's `image` SDC to comply with best practices, and document those best practices
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3468944 · 6 contributors · 26 comments
  • Followers: 12

Summary

The built-in image SDC that ships with Drupal Canvas (formerly Experience Builder) has been restructured to follow SDC best practices. The redundant nested image object prop was removed in favour of flat top-level props (src, alt, width, height, sizes, loading). The template now correctly outputs the auto-injected attributes variable, making the component compatible with lazy-loading APIs, ARIA injection, and other systems that attach HTML attributes. Responsive image support was also added via srcset generation and a lazy loading default. The component is now marked noUi: true, signalling that it is a developer building block for inclusion in other SDCs rather than a user-selectable element in the Canvas component browser.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: The canvas:image SDC prop schema changed from a single nested image object to flat top-level props (src, alt, width, height, sizes, loading); callers must update with blocks and template references accordingly.

Technical details

The original image.component.yml wrapped all image data inside a single image object prop, accessed in the Twig template as {{ image.src }}, {{ image.alt }}, etc. As noted in the issue thread by pdureau, this created a redundant nesting layer: the component and its sole prop described exactly the same thing. The fix (landed in #3535453) promotes all properties to the top level, so the template accesses {{ src }}, {{ alt }}, {{ width }}, {{ height }}, {{ sizes }}, and {{ loading }} directly.

The Twig template in components/image/image.twig was rewritten to output {{ attributes }} at the bottom of the <img> tag, consuming the Attribute object that SDC automatically injects into every component. Two custom Twig filters, getWidth/getHeight (to derive intrinsic dimensions from the source when not supplied) and toSrcSet (to generate a srcset attribute), were added alongside file_url() to resolve Drupal stream wrapper URIs. The loading prop defaults to lazy and sizes defaults to auto 100vw.

The noUi: true flag in the component definition tells Canvas to exclude this component from the editor's component browser. A code comment in the YAML explains the deliberate decision not to reuse the json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/image JSON Schema reference: since this SDC is composed into other SDCs, flat top-level props give a better developer experience than a single nested object would. The issue discussion also produced a concrete follow-up: defining attributes explicitly as a prop in SDC YAML (as several XB components had done) is incorrect because SDC already injects it automatically, and using a PHP namespace as a prop type is not JSON Schema compliant (tracked in #3470575).

Upgrade

If you have custom SDCs that include canvas:image and access props with the old nested pattern, flatten the references:

{# Before #}
<img src="{{ image.src }}" alt="{{ image.alt }}" width="{{ image.width }}" height="{{ image.height }}">

{# After — props are now top-level #}
{% include 'canvas:image' with { src: ..., alt: ..., width: ..., height: ... } %}

Also remove any explicit attributes prop definition from your own SDC YAML files if it uses a PHP namespace type such as Drupal\Core\Template\Attribute; SDC injects attributes automatically and that type string is not JSON Schema compliant.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: August 19, 2024

Key contributors

  • pdureau
  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • ctrladel (True Summit)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

Collaboration

pdureau provided the critical technical grounding for the whole issue. In comment #5 they explained that every SDC template already receives an auto-injected attributes object, documented why the nested image prop was architecturally redundant, and argued that plain HTML elements do not need SDC wrappers at all because Drupal already has #type => 'html_tag' renderables. That analysis directly shaped the final component design: flat props, attributes output, and the noUi: true marker that separates developer building blocks from end-user components. ctrladel added a counterpoint in comment #16 defending the single-prop nesting as a future-proofing technique and noting that explicit attributes output felt like an SDC antipattern inherited from Drupal's render layer, a view that lauriii ultimately weighed in on to close the issue.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3549885: Prevent deleting Code Components if there are usages in forward revisions
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3549885 · 3 contributors · 8 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Deleting a Code Component in Canvas now also checks whether that component is referenced in any forward (pending, non-default) revision, such as content staged inside a Workspace. Previously, only auto-save storage and default revisions were checked; forward revisions were silently ignored. This meant that publishing a workspace after deleting a component it used would cause those pages to render the component fallback instead of the intended content. The fix prevents the deletion from being permitted when the component is in use in any latest revision.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The delete access check lives in VisibleWhenDisabledCanvasConfigEntityAccessControlHandler::checkAccess(). Before this fix it ran two AccessResult::forbiddenIf() calls chained with ->orIf(): one querying RevisionAuditEnum::AutoSave (auto-save storage) and one querying RevisionAuditEnum::Default (current/default revisions). A third ->orIf() check using RevisionAuditEnum::Latest was added, which translates to $query->latestRevision() inside ComponentAudit::getContentRevisionIdsUsingComponentIds(). This covers any non-default, pending revision (most notably content that has been edited inside a Workspace but not yet published). The ordering is intentional: auto-save is checked first because it is cheapest; default revision next; latest revision last, as it is the most expensive and only relevant when the preceding checks pass.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: October 2, 2025

Key contributors

  • effulgentsia
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3544213: Harden XB content dependency calculation infrastructure to not cause a PHP fatal error when content entities have disappeared whose deletion should have been prevented
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3544213 · 3 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

Deleting a media entity that was referenced inside a Canvas component tree could cause a PHP fatal TypeError when anything tried to calculate content dependencies for that component tree, such as running drush content:export or certain update hooks. The crash happened because Canvas's dependency calculation code did not defend against the case where a referenced entity no longer exists. This has been hardened so that missing entity references are skipped gracefully rather than causing a fatal error. A related crash in the ComputedUrlWithQueryString data type (which produced an AssertionError instead of a TypeError) was also fixed: it now returns an empty URL with the deleted entity's cache tags so that any cached empty result is invalidated if the entity is ever restored.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The fatal originated in ReferenceFieldTypePropExpression::calculateDependencies() and ReferenceFieldPropExpression::calculateDependencies(). Both methods call EntityReferenceFieldItemListInterface::referencedEntities() to collect referenced entities and then map over them to build dependencies['content']. When a referenced entity has been deleted, earlier code paths could reach this with a null entity value; the PHP typed closure fn (FieldableEntityInterface $entity) then threw a TypeError. The fix switches to relying on referencedEntities() returning only fully loadable entities (deleted entries are absent rather than null), and adds an \assert(Inspector::assertAllObjects(..., FieldableEntityInterface::class)) guard to catch regressions in development.

The companion fix (MR 1264) addressed ComputedUrlWithQueryString, which encountered the same deleted-entity condition through a different path. When the resolved URL value is NULL and the prop expression is a ReferencePropExpressionInterface, the class now calls TypedDataHelper::getDeletedReferencedEntityCacheability() to produce a CacheableMetadata carrying the deleted entity's cache tag (entity_type_id:id), then returns the partially-built GeneratedUrl instead of asserting. The cache tag ensures the cached empty URL is invalidated if the entity is recreated. Parallel discussion in the thread pointed to the deeper core-side problem of preventing deletion of entities that are active config dependencies, tracked separately in the core issue #3532615.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 1, 2025

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3583854: [upstream] Validate LanguageConfigOverrides targeting Canvas config entities
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3583854 · 1 contributor · 7 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

Canvas now validates language config overrides (LanguageConfigOverride) for its config entity types (ContentTemplate and PageRegion) against config schema and constraints. Drupal core has never validated these overrides beyond checking that values are scalars, arrays, or null, meaning an invalid translation (for example, a boolean stored where a URI is expected) could silently corrupt a site. Canvas fills this gap with two complementary mechanisms: a test-time event subscriber that throws a ConfigException when an invalid override is saved, and a runtime entity constraint that surfaces violations through the standard entity validation API.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New CanvasConfigEntityTranslationsAreValidConstraint (plugin ID CanvasConfigEntityTranslationsAreValid) constraint added to ContentTemplate and PageRegion config entity types; ConfigTranslationSupportHooks::TRANSLATABLE_ENTITY_TYPE_IDS public constant added.

Technical details

Drupal core's LanguageConfigOverride::save() only calls StorableConfigBase::validateValue(), which rejects objects and resources but ignores schema. Core's ConfigSchemaChecker (the test-time strict validator) explicitly skips non-default collections, because overrides are partial and cannot be validated alone. The fix merges the override data onto the base config via NestedArray::mergeDeepArray() before validating, replicating what Config::setOverriddenData() does at read time.

Two mechanisms are added. First, LanguageConfigOverrideSchemaChecker (@internal, test-only) is an EventSubscriberInterface that listens to LanguageConfigOverrideEvents::SAVE_OVERRIDE. When a save targets a Canvas config entity (identified by canvas.content_template. or canvas.page_region. config name prefixes), it merges the override onto the base config and validates via TypedConfigManagerInterface. Violations result in a ConfigException. It is registered as an event subscriber in both CanvasKernelTestBase and FunctionalTestBase, mirroring how core registers ConfigSchemaChecker. Second, CanvasConfigEntityTranslationsAreValidConstraint (plugin ID CanvasConfigEntityTranslationsAreValid) is added to ContentTemplate and PageRegion entity types in hook_entity_type_alter() via ConfigTranslationSupportHooks. Its validator (CanvasConfigEntityTranslationsAreValidConstraintValidator) iterates over all configured languages, merges each language's override onto the base config, validates via typed config, and surfaces violations through the entity validation API. The canonical list of translatable Canvas config entity types is centralized in ConfigTranslationSupportHooks::TRANSLATABLE_ENTITY_TYPE_IDS.

Both mechanisms are explicitly temporary workarounds for the long-standing core issue #2270399 (a @todo in LanguageConfigOverride::save() for over a decade), and carry @todo Remove when ... comments pointing to that issue.

Upgrade

Tests that save LanguageConfigOverride values for Canvas config entities (ContentTemplate, PageRegion) now fail with a ConfigException if the merged result (base config + override) violates config schema or constraints. Ensure all such saves in tests use valid values. Example of what now throws:

// Saving a non-URI string into a URI-typed prop now throws ConfigException
// in test environments (where LanguageConfigOverrideSchemaChecker is active):
$this->saveConfigEntityTranslation($template, 'nl', $nl_values_with_invalid_uri);
// → ConfigException: Validation of the "canvas.content_template...." LanguageConfigOverride failed: ...

At runtime (non-test), the CanvasConfigEntityTranslationsAreValidConstraint constraint on ContentTemplate and PageRegion means that calling $entity->validate() will now return violations for each language whose override produces an invalid merged config.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 9, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591789: Deleting a content entity translation leaves its auto-save snapshot behind; publishing resurrects the deleted translation
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591789 · 1 contributor · 5 comments

Summary

On Canvas sites using content translation, deleting a single translation of a page left that translation's unpublished draft (auto-save snapshot) behind in the store. The next time the page's pending changes were published, Canvas silently reapplied the stale snapshot and recreated the deleted translation, undoing the editorial decision without any warning or conflict report. After this fix, deleting a translation also discards its draft immediately, so publish produces the expected result.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The auto-save store holds one snapshot per langcode. ApiAutoSaveController::applyAutoSaveTranslationSnapshots() iterates every stored snapshot for an entity's translation group and overlays each one onto a freshly loaded entity; when it finds a langcode that no longer exists it falls through to addTranslation(), which is the resurrection path. Canvas already implemented hook_entity_delete() in AutoSaveHooks::entityDelete() to discard the entire snapshot group when a whole entity is deleted. The single-translation case was not handled because hook_entity_translation_delete() had no implementation. The fix adds AutoSaveHooks::entityTranslationDelete(), which calls AutoSaveManager::delete($translation) with only the deleted translation object, discarding just that langcode's snapshot while leaving sibling translations' drafts untouched. The fix is covered by testDeletingTranslationDiscardsItsSnapshot() in ContentEntityTranslationPropagationTest, which asserts that after removing a Spanish translation the EN snapshot survives, and that a subsequent publish call does not restore the ES translation to the entity.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 8, 2026

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591700: Support `content-entity-reference` props in CLI and Workbench
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591700 · 1 contributor · 4 comments

Summary

The Canvas module now fully supports content-entity-reference (CER) props throughout the developer workflow. Before this change, components that referenced a Drupal content entity via a CER prop could not be previewed reliably in Workbench, pulled content could lose entity reference data, and AI coding agents lacked enough context to work with these props. After this change: the Workbench sidebar shows a live entity picker for each CER prop so developers can select the preview entity per prop; page, region, and content template previews also resolve CER props before rendering; the CLI can pull and push dataDependencies.entityFields from component.yml; a new cer-expressions context provider generates a browsable list of field expressions grouped by entity type and bundle; and a new cer-preview context provider resolves and prints the actual prop shape a component will receive at runtime, which AI agents can use to understand what the component code should consume.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New POST /canvas/api/v0/ui/content-entity-reference/preview/{entity_type}/{entity} endpoint and new ApiService methods previewContentEntityReferenceFields, listContentEntityReferenceEntityTypes, and fetchContentEntityReferenceFields.

Technical details

On the Drupal side, a new controller action ApiUiContentEntityReferenceControllers::preview() is registered at POST /canvas/api/v0/ui/content-entity-reference/preview/{entity_type}/{entity}. It accepts a JSON body with an entityFields map (prop names to field expression arrays), parses and coalesces those expressions via JavaScriptComponent::parseAndCoalesceEntityFieldExpressions, evaluates them against the loaded entity via JsComponent::buildReferencePayload, and returns resolved prop values. Access is guarded by the Canvas UI access check, OAuth authentication, and the entity-level entity.view permission.

On the CLI side, pullAgentsContext was refactored from a single monolithic function into a provider-based system. Three AgentsContextProvider implementations live under agents-context-providers/: content-templates (existing behavior, now the default provider), content-entity-reference-expressions (alias cer-expressions), and content-entity-reference-preview (alias cer-preview, not default). The agents-context command now requires either a provider name or --all; calling it without either prints help and exits. The cer-expressions provider calls listContentEntityReferenceEntityTypes and fetchContentEntityReferenceFields on ApiService, walks nested reference fields up to four levels deep via collectNestedFieldJobs, and writes content-entity-reference-expressions.json. The cer-preview provider loads local component.yml metadata, reads dataDependencies.entityFields, fetches a preview entity suggestion for each CER prop, and calls the new previewContentEntityReferenceFields endpoint to print the resolved prop shape. The pull task now preserves dataDependencies.entityFields when writing local component.yml files, and the Metadata type gains an optional dataDependencies field. The validate command is refactored to use a new validateComponents function that accepts the full discovery result and an optional ApiService; CER prop validation against the live site now happens here when credentials are available.

In the Workbench (packages/workbench), App.tsx derives the set of CER preview props with getContentEntityReferencePropPreviews, which reads x-allowed-entity-type-id and x-allowed-bundle from each prop's JSON Schema alongside dataDependencies.entityFields. A PreviewEntityPicker component is rendered in the sidebar for each CER prop. During default (non-mock) component preview rendering, groupEntityFieldsByProp collects selected entity IDs, and fetchResolvedContentEntityReferenceFields calls the new endpoint for each group; resolved values are merged into the spec with applyResolvedToElementsOfType. For page, region, and content template previews, fetchResolvedContentEntityReferenceFieldsForSpec walks the full spec and resolves CER props for all components in it.

Upgrade

If you have automation that calls npx canvas agents-context without arguments, update it. The command now shows help and exits when no provider is specified. To reproduce the previous behavior (generating prop sources and view modes), use:

npx canvas agents-context content-templates
# or run all default providers:
npx canvas agents-context --all

To generate the new CER expressions file:

npx canvas agents-context cer-expressions

To inspect resolved CER prop values for a component:

npx canvas agents-context cer-preview article-card

In component.yml, CER props must carry both x-allowed-entity-type-id and x-allowed-bundle, and the prop must not appear in required. Add field expressions under dataDependencies.entityFields.<propName>:

props:
  properties:
    featuredArticle:
      title: Featured article
      type: object
      $ref: json-schema-definitions://canvas.module/content-entity-reference
      x-allowed-entity-type-id: node
      x-allowed-bundle: article
dataDependencies:
  entityFields:
    featuredArticle:
      - ℹ︎␜entity:node:article␝title␞␟value
      - ℹ︎␜entity:node:article␝path␞␟alias

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 18, 2026

Key contributors

  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • balintbrews (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3591745: Enabling content translation of Canvas pages results in an unsupported state (asymmetrical translations) by default: force the known valid state if the field is marked as translatable
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Canvas
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3591745 · 1 contributor · 12 comments

Summary

When site builders enabled content translation on Canvas pages, the standard Drupal "Content language and translation" settings form presented checkboxes for both the component input values and the component tree, allowing asymmetrical configurations that Canvas does not support. This fix enforces the only valid state: component input values are translatable, the component tree is shared across languages. The form now hides the column-group checkboxes and renames the field label to "Component input values" to clarify what is actually being translated. A post-update hook (canvas_post_update_0022) repairs any site that previously reached an invalid configuration. Going forward, the correct state is also set automatically on module install and rejected at config import time.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Database update: Post-update hook canvas_post_update_0022_enforce_symmetrical_canvas_page_components_translation creates or corrects the components base field override to enforce symmetrical translation settings.
  • Config change: New core.base_field_override.canvas_page.canvas_page.components config object is created on install and enforced by a post-update hook; config schema constraints and a config import validator reject the asymmetrical translation_sync combination.

Technical details

The components base field of the canvas_page entity type stores both the component tree (which instances exist, in what order) and per-instance input values. Content translation's FieldTranslationSynchronizer uses the translation_sync third-party setting on a BaseFieldOverride to decide which column groups to synchronize across translations. The only supported combination is inputs => 'inputs' (translatable) and tree => '0' (shared). Previously the UI exposed checkboxes for both, so any user could accidentally enable the unsupported asymmetrical mode.

Enforcement is now layered across four surfaces. First, ModuleHooks::afterBuildCanvasPageLanguageSettings() removes the components column-group sub-checkboxes from the form, renames the field label, and ModuleHooks::validateCanvasPageLanguageSettings() (a new form validator) hard-codes the symmetrical values into form state before the settings are saved. Second, a hook_modules_installed implementation in ContentTranslationHooks calls ComponentTreeFieldSymmetricalTranslationSynchronizer::ensureSymmetricalCanvasPageComponents() to create or update the BaseFieldOverride whenever either canvas or content_translation is installed while the other is already present. Third, a new config schema file canvas_symmetrical_translations_only.schema.yml defines Choice constraints on the translation_sync mapping, making the valid state machine-checkable. Fourth, SymmetricalTranslationsConfigImportValidator (registered as an event subscriber on ConfigImporterEvent) validates the staged override before any config import and logs an error to abort the import if the tree column group is set to translatable. The post-update hook canvas_post_update_0022 handles four distinct cases: no content_translation installed (no-op), an existing override with invalid settings (corrected), an existing override already valid (no-op), and content_translation present but no override yet (created). All of this is marked @todo Remove in issue #3571130, which tracks a planned core fix that would make the form-level workaround unnecessary.

Upgrade

Sites running Canvas with content_translation enabled should run database updates to trigger canvas_post_update_0022, which will create or fix the core.base_field_override.canvas_page.canvas_page.components config object:

drush updatedb

If you manage config with Config Management (CMI/recipes), be aware that importing a staged override with translation_sync.tree set to 'tree' (the asymmetrical value) will now be rejected at import time with a message containing "Canvas Pages support only symmetrical translations". Correct the staged config to translation_sync: { inputs: inputs, tree: '0' } before importing.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 29, 2026

Key contributors

  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • lauriii (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
